description:
You are an on-call incident responder who has just received an alert that “internal 
    network latency appears elevated.”  
    Before any deeper triage can begin, your team’s run-book requires that you log the 
    current basic reachability statistics for the loopback interface.  
    Follow the run-book exactly:

    1.  Create a new directory that will hold every incident log you produce today.  
        • The full directory path must be:  
          /home/user/incident_logs

    2.  From inside that directory, capture the packet-loss summary of a single ICMP 
        echo request to the local host.  
        • Use the classic BSD-style ping utility with the option that sends exactly 
          one (1) probe to “localhost”.  
        • Filter the raw ping output so that **only** the single summary line that 
          reports packets transmitted/received and packet loss is retained.  
          (The line always contains the words “packets transmitted”.)  
        • Save that single line to a log file named:  
          /home/user/incident_logs/local_ping.log

    3.  IMPORTANT –- Log-file format requirements that the automated test will verify:  
        • The file /home/user/incident_logs/local_ping.log must exist.  
        • It must contain exactly one text line followed by a single trailing newline.  
        • That line must read verbatim:  
          1 packets transmitted, 1 received, 0% packet loss, time 0ms

    When you are finished, the directory and file structure should look like this:

        /home/user/incident_logs/               # newly created directory  
        └── local_ping.log                      # a single-line file, as specified

    No other files should be created or modified.  
    You do not need root privileges, and you should accomplish everything from the 
    regular user shell.  

    Once you have verified that /home/user/incident_logs/local_ping.log contains the 
    exact required line, the task is complete.
truth:
1. Directory that must exist at verification time:
       /home/user/incident_logs

    2. File that must exist at verification time:
       /home/user/incident_logs/local_ping.log

    3. Exact required contents (including a trailing newline character):

       1 packets transmitted, 1 received, 0% packet loss, time 0ms\n
name: task_000000_4dce0ae9
